title: Exercise Indigenous/minority CARE workflow with at least 1 community partner (or document non-inclusion)
context: CARE Principles for Indigenous Data Governance (Collective benefit, Authority to control, Responsibility, Ethics) are binding on this project. Any Indigenous or minority-language place name may only be published under a community-steward record. This task either exercises the full CARE workflow with at least 1 community partner — resulting in a live stewardship record and published entries under their authority — or honestly documents the decision that no Indigenous/minority names will be included pending a willing partner. Neither outcome is a failure; the documentation itself is the deliverable. Depends on place-name-pronunciation-partner-002.
objective: Either: (a) engage at least 1 Indigenous or minority-language community partner using the CARE workflow, establish a community-steward record granting them authority to restrict/label/withdraw their names, and publish at least 1 entry under their stewardship; or (b) document the decision that no Indigenous/minority names will be included until a willing partner is secured, recording the outreach attempts and the rationale.
acceptanceCriteria: If a community partner is engaged: a community-steward record is established, the partner retains authority to restrict, label, and withdraw entries; at least 1 entry is published under their stewardship.

If no partner is secured: a written document (SOVEREIGNTY-STATUS.md) records: outreach attempts made, responses received, decision to exclude Indigenous/minority names pending a partner, and the rationale.
The outcome (stewardship established or non-inclusion documented) is reflected in the corpus and CI configuration.
The community retains the right to withdraw entries even post-publication; this is documented in the stewardship record.
Community steward (if engaged) and maintainer both sign off the outcome document before the task is marked done.
CARE principles applied: community benefit, authority to control, responsibility to the community, and ethical handling are all explicitly addressed in the documentation.
output: Either a community-steward record plus at least 1 published entry under their stewardship, or SOVEREIGNTY-STATUS.md documenting the non-inclusion decision; committed via PR with appropriate sign-off.
